methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
WB Group is a pre-IPO stage defense and security company founded by Piotr Wojciechowski. The broader Defense & Security sector comprises 21 peer companies across public and private markets, including 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers spanning companies like Anduril Industries, Scale AI, Saronic, Flock Safety, and Shield AI.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The sector contains sufficient overall peers, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded zero datapoints meeting the three-company threshold required for robust median analysis. Applying an all-sector median would introduce excessive heterogeneity across business models and maturity profiles and would not produce a defensible estimate.

Without sufficient narrow peer anchors, this analysis cannot triangulate a supported valuation range using comparable company methodology. A complete valuation would require alternative approaches—such as discounted cash flow analysis with disclosed financials, transaction precedent analysis if available, or qualitative assessment relative to the identified private peer set—paired with primary diligence on WB Group's revenue, growth trajectory, and competitive positioning within defense and security. The key uncertainty remains the absence of narrowly comparable transaction or trading data at the pre-IPO stage in this specialized sector.
